name: Dr. Anjali Karira
onlinePlatform: Have noticed on Practo,( but it's good to ask.)
address: Dr. Farooquis Polyclinic - Bandra West, St. Martin's Road, Off Turner Road, Landmark : Near Popley Jewelers

reviews.text: Its too soon to tell. Although up until now, she's been very approachable, one doesn't have to hesitate, listens patiently and is interrogative. Openly discusses medicine options.
She's absolutely amazing. Listens patiently, very knowledgeable about ADHD meds, extremely sweet. She also tries to listen and give advice, not just prescribe meds.

name: Dr. Sasha Raikhy
address: Possitive Vibes, Safdarjung enclave, Delhi

reviews.text: The doc was very comfortable to talk to and listened patiently. I didn't mention ADHD at all. The doctor herself said it might be that so I don't think there is problem regarding stigma. The management at the clinic though has been less than ideal in my experience.

It was good. But they were initially forcing me to do a mental therapy for ADHD in a machine.

But we finally went off with the stimulants and its working fine. 
I had severe anxiety issues and major depressive disorder so she is helping me with that as well.
Overall, i am very satisfied.

One thing is that, she does not explain what each medicine individually does, like how does x stimulant work, what ADHD actually is, and etc.

name: Dr. Naveen Jayaram
onlinePlatform: Sakra world hospital official website. Or direct whatsapp.
address: Sakra world hospital, Bellandur AND GHC, Whitefield (Both Bangalore)

reviews.text: Friendly doctor
Friendly doctor

name: Porrselvi
address: UBHC, Saligramam, Chennai

reviews.text: I had an amazing experience with her. As someone with extreme therapy trauma from horrendous prior experiences, she made me feel safe and comfortable and most of all we had a good time talking to each other. She's extremely friendly and takes the time to teach us about ADHD and how the brain works. She doesn't care about society or anything they have to say and she doesn't want us to care either because our mental health is our priority. Trust me, with her, it cannot go wrong. 100% recommend.

I have mixed feelings about Porrselvi. To her credit, she is friendly and easy to talk to, she is affirming of Adult ADHD and has a good understanding of how it manifests, and she has no stigma about stimulants. She is also helpful with comorbities of ADHD, like OCD and anxiety. In all these respects, she is great. Unfortunately, though, I don't think she is very helpful with guiding someone to learn coping techniques for ADHD symptoms. Sure, she suggests for coping techniques for executive dysfunction and anxiety, but for me, the fact that adopting those coping techniques is difficult is why I want to get help. But I hit a wall with her there - she didn't seem to understand that suggestions of "doing so-and-so will help" are somewhat pointless if doing that so-and-so is just exceptionally difficult. Intrusive anxious thoughts won't just go away just by willing it.

Dr. Rastogi is a very thorough and friendly professional- he takes personal concerns around medication and tracking seriously, is careful when prescribing multiple medications, and encourages a degree of personal agency and responsibility in your treatment.

name: Dr. Arun B Nair
address: Keshavadaspuram, aiswarya nagar

reviews.text: Very good doctor , highly renowned in kerala

name: Dr. Dharmendra Singh
address: Disha Neuropsychiatry clinic CR park Delhi

reviews.text: Dr. Dharmendra is friendly and supportive, though He's quite good at optimising medication, I'd suggest going to a clinical psychologist for CBT

name: Dr. Arindam Mondal
address: Apollo Gleneagles, Kolkata

reviews.text: He was alright, his manner was a little brusque and he said some insensitive things about ADHD and seems to hold some common misconceptions that people with ADHD cannot do well academically or that stimulant medication makes you 'clever'.

name: Dr. Shivani Agarwal
address: VIMHANS, Delhi

reviews.text: She was very empathic, heard out patiently, asked a lot of questions, treated the patient as a whole person and not a set of symptoms. I have heard good feedback about her from others who have been diagnosed with ADHD by her.
I have not visted them myself but their profile claims that they're neurodivergent and queer affirmative and they also appear to be interested in ending mental health stigma

Neurodivergent affirming, is very considerate towards self diagnosis as well and willing to work based on the client's lived experience rather than just depending on clinical diagnosis/labels, also she is very understanding of the fact that we know our brains best and is willing to hear and understand any explanations that we might have come up with on our own rather than blatantly ignoring all the research we often do as "dr google is bad"
- very friendly -good understanding of adult adhd -no stigma around medication

name: Dr. Anand Balan
address: 14, Sambasivam St, Parthasarathi Puram, T. Nagar, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600017

reviews.text: Simple, relatively pain-free. I told him the litany of my symptoms, and he concurred with me that I infact had adult ADD, and put me on methylphenidate.

I went in, and described all my symptoms. I already knew I probably have ADHD, so I just told him everything about that. He asked me a lot of questions, and finally gave the diagnosis. He recognises Adult ADHD and inattentive-type, and he's friendly and listens to what you're saying. He also answers your questions, especially for family members who don't know what ADHD is. He also has no problems prescribing methylphenidate, but I feel like he's too lax with prescribing stimulants; that is, he did tell me about possible loss of sleep, but he did not focus enough about the danger of developing a dependence on methylphenidate, or tell me about how to wean myself out of it if needed. He's also a psychiatrist, not a psychotherapist, so he will not help you with making lifestyle changes or with ADHD-caused anxiety and things like that, he will only prescribe stimulants for ADHD.

name: Meena / Hijab
address: ReACH Psychiatry & Counselling Center #319, 7th main, 2nd block, HRBR layout, CMR Road, Kalyan Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560043

reviews.text:
I'm not sure whether they did a standardized assessment for diagnosing, as the clinical psychologist just did the diagnosis in the session by asking questions, and then emailed me the diagnosis that I have adhd and later asked me to take an assessment questionnaire which went into a lot more details.

They seemed friendly and had good understanding of adult ADHD and medication though.

even if you do not go to this guy you may take a recommendation from him for good doctor in your state.

The doctor doesn't have stigma about stimulant and even prescribed me on the second visit. She has MRCPsych and completed masters in UK so takes ADHD seriously.

reviews.text: She's well-versed and understanding about everything adult ADHD. I was diagnosed by her. I am not sure how the therapy experience will be, but I do know she primarily practices CBT.

HIGHLY RECOMMENDED. Especially for ADHD. She is the most practical, solution oriented doc I've come across. She specializes in ADHD, but is a clinical psychologist otherwise as well so you can visit for all kinds off issues. In my experience her advice on all life aspects is extremely helpful. The affordability is a nice cherry on the top. I've never felt invalidated here.

reviews.text: The doctor is a bit blunt and not diplomatic, but sharp in her observations and quick to get a hold of heart of the issue. The psychologist is the best of the two i have so far visited, patient with me, and focuses on making you have the tools to deal with your own problems, which builds less dependence on the doctor. Therapy is CBT based and has helped me manage my anxiety around my diagnosis. Full disclosure: i went for depression and anxiety. Got a self referred test done for ADHD, but they did not prescribe me medication as they think PTSD and depression are playing a more significant role in my case.

reviews.text: very Good

name: Dr. Arnab Ghosh Hazra
address: Zenith, ILS

reviews.text:
The doctor has a lot of positive reviews on Google and Practo. When I went there he was a bit smug and kept asking me why I didn't show a psychiatrist earlier.etc.

When I told him I was having trouble focusing and a couple more lines, he immediately prescribed Inspiral, Quogress and Cognicure and asked me to consult his wife first for a diet for ADD.

The whole interaction was barely 5-7 mins long and I was rushed out.

Clinic fees is 800, but he asked me to only do online for further consultation which costs 1500.

Would not recommend.

In the end said this medicine causes loss of appetite and you look like you need that...( I'm a little overweight, but not an appropriate remark)

name: Dr. Jamila Koshy
address: Baptist Hospital, Bellary Rd, Vinayakanagar, Hebbal, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560024

reviews.text:
She was very good. My therapist referred her and I started with a teleconsultation. Booked through the website and we did a Whatsapp call for nearly an hour. She asked me to come for follow up after I said I'm in Bangalore. Did a long session in person too, and asked me to do medical check ups. 3rd visit she prescribed Inspiral 10 mg once a day. 

She did not have any stigma for sure. Not sure about recognizing only inattentive ADHD, but I think she would. She was quite friendly.

Very positive experience.

- Very understanding towards adult adhd
- I went in for inattentive only reasons, but it turns out I do have both. So I'm not sure, but she most likely does recognise inattentive adhd.
- Had no stigma about stimulants, however also pointed out (rightfully) that I had more issues that needed to be dealt with, before medication could be properly effective, and would prescribe me my medication then.

Go with clear expectations of what you want, have a clear conversation and preferably have someone to vouch for you. Also very queer/trans friendly. I'm not the easiest patient to deal with, and she was very friendly the whole time

name: Dr. Ritwick Chatterjee
address: Office No. 1, 1st floor, Westend Shopping Complex, In front of Dmart, Aundh, Pune-411007

reviews.text: Highly recommend this doctor. He also noticed my anxiety and obsessive nature and suggested meds to get to my baseline (trait vs state) before jumping to an adhd conclusion. No stigma for stimulant medication. Really patient, friendly, cool and will try to understand the environmental and biological aspects that could be affecting you.

- super patient
- listens and chats with patience and interest
- no stigma about stimulants
- can be a slight bit gimmicky with his quick analysis of yourself, but that's some party tricks they need to have esp since they also deal in children's mental health
- overall a very good experience after having had an offline consultation

address: Sai Health Care, Plot Number 137, First Floor, Unit 6, Landmark: Near Air Port Chaka, Bhubaneswar

reviews.text: They took time to listen to my case. And they recognised adult ADHD. They also sent stimulant meds over courier after online consultation.

he was really friendly, he let me speak about my symptoms without interrupting and heard patiently, first we agreed upon a one-to-one conversation and then he called my father out to explain what was happening to me. He gives a lot of time to the patients. He said he would diagnose me but he didn't about which I will ask him on my next visit. He gave me options to choose for stimulants and non-stimulants and discounted on the meds

I was a bit anxious about how to start the conversation, but he made it really easy. He asked a bit about me to break the ice, we had some casual chat, and then gradually moved into discussing my concerns. I shared all my symptoms and told him I suspected it might be ADHD. I had noted everything in my phone and just read them out to him one by one. Within 10 minutes, he was fairly confident that it was ADHD (specifically ADD, without the hyperactivity).

He was calm, reassuring, and explained that it’s manageable, and quite a few people have it. He gave me options for both stimulant and non-stimulant medications, since I wasn’t fully comfortable jumping into meds right away. He also gave me some advice on managing without medication.

Time was a bit limited, I think he had about 20–30 minutes per session due to other patients, but overall, it was a solid experience. He clearly understood ADHD well and even pointed out how some doctors often misdiagnose it as just anxiety or depression. I was prepared to go to 3–4 clinics if needed to get a proper diagnosis, but he got it right on the first try. Hope this info helps someone.

The doctor charges 600 INR per visit (one of the lowest fees ever)
The doctor recognises ADD
The doctor prescribes stimulants
The doctor will mail you stimulants too 
I have also recommended the doctor to a lot of my friends and they got diagnosed too :)

No stigma on meds, asked me what's going on, and once diagnosed asked if I wanted to start with stimulants or non-stimulants. He has his own person to dispatch medication through courier. He did very well understand adult ADHD and it was an easy talk and extremely cheap consultation. Greatly recommended!
Non-judgemental, very attentive, listens. Took adult adhd seriously. Also acknowledged Inattentive adhd. No stigma about stimulant medication

I should have written all my symptoms beforehand but as i wasn't expecting much i didn't bothered (biggest mistake) and in panic my dyslexic symptoms which are very much visible only gor noticed. Though he has referred me to his psychologist colleague who specialises in learning disability therapy. I'll make sure to write down my symptoms for this session and after my visit will update her contact too.

reviews.text: Dr. Kaustubh Joag is one of the most patient people I have met. I didn't know about ADHD until I saw myself overwhelmingly agreeing with various comments and memes online. (Yeah, weird, but ADHD wasn't a thing when I was a kid.) I was consulting with Dr. Joag for anxiety issues and asked him about the possibility of ADHD. He was quite open minded despite the fact that I was 39. He administered the test and, sure enough, I did have mild-to-moderate ADHD.

Very too the point and calm explanations well about the problems and how to deal with it, too the point he speaks less but whatever he speaks you should note it down as that can help change your life in a positive manner.

They're very friendly, but also not excessively so- they see through what you're saying clear as day.

They prescribe stimulants and it's not a hassle to get them to prescribe stimulants, but they are kind of conservative in that they're not extremely pro-stimulants. But I strongly believe they know what they're doing and they act in your best interests, even if he hesitates to do so.

reviews.text: good experience , makes u feel comfortable as we might have trouble starting, explains the causes and solutions clearly

name: Dr. Philip John
onlinePlatform: None. Consultation is done by contacting his clinic - Peejay's Policlinic.
address: PEEJAYS Policlinic and Child Guidance Clinic @ the Neurocenter, AISHA ROAD, NH Byepass, Next to Gold Souk, VYTTILA, Kochi-19, Kerala

reviews.text:
You are assessed based on your entire psychiatric history by the team of specialists at the clinic to check for any neurodevelopmental disorder and other conditions you might have. (Not just ADHD). This could include language and speech disorders, learning disabilities, OCD, etc.
This whole report is then forwarded to the neuropsychiatrist(s) present- Dr Philip John, in my case (Although other psychiatrists are working under him, he seems to be the doc in charge of most of the cases). This consultation is only done online, as far as I'm aware.
He then provides you with a whole neuroscientific analysis of your entire brain based on the report and gives you an explanation for behaviours you have exhibited throughout your life.
He also prescribes the required medical regimen.

The entire experience was very professional and friendly. I had doubts on whether they do assessments for adults(most of the advertising was centred around child psychiatry), but it seems they have adult cases very often. Zero stigma.
Pros: They take your case in an all-encompassing lens, without just focusing on your ADHD. The approach roots out all co-morbidities and disorders.
Cons: My total expenditure ended up being a bit high (6.5k for the whole visit + around 3k for the meds).

Details on how to book a consultation are on their website.

name: Dr. Esther Suresh
address: Rofecha 60/94, Block V, V Block, 600040, Anna Nagar, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600040

reviews.text:
They were friendly and have a broader perspective than just a diagnosis
They have a good understanding of adult adhd and recognise the inattentive type
No stigma about stimulant medication but only prescribes for 2 months for regular checkups for addiction

name: Dr. Jai R Ram
address: Apollo G

reviews.text:
He is a very good doctor , very understanding and comfortable to talk to . I believe he has very good knowledge about adult adhd, he recognises inattentive adhd. 
I personally liked the doctor because he is very understanding and it was easy for me to connect to him.

name: Anindita Adya
onlinePlatform: Usually Google Meet or Zoom for sessions

reviews.text: I was referred to her by my former therapist to evaluate possible ADHD, and I ended up deciding to continue therapy with Anindita. She was extremely patient, kind, and seemed to understand the "neurodivergent experience" in a better way than my previous therapist. For the assessment process, she carefully explained each step and why it is being done, answered questions thoroughly. Once we began discussing treatment, she spoke about CBT-based intervention for ADHD specifically but also emotional issues that had come up as a result of ADHD that needs to be addressed in therapy. She provided a lot of clarity about medication including stimulant-medication without any such stigma or discouragement about it. She was also listening when I said that I didn't want to be on medication, said that it is important that I make an informed decision

name: Dr. Jyoti Sangle
address: Private Practice (Online & Offline)/ Holy Spirit Hospital Mumbai/ Dr. LH Hiranandani Hospital

reviews.text: Dr. Jyoti Sangle is kind and understanding. One of my friend suggested her after getting her own ADHD diagnosis fron Dr. Jyoti. After consulting 3 other psychiatrists in the past, she is the only doctor to prescribe me medications meant exactly for ADHD while constantly checking with me on side effects to see its effectiveness. She also values your opinions and does not have any stigma regarding ADHD. I regret not consulting her earlier. I would suggest you to spend money on doctors like her who actually value your thoughts and understand your condition, rather than wasting money on doctors who dismiss your symptoms. I would highly recommend Dr. Jyoti Sangle for your ADHD diagnosis.
Very professional doctor with good understanding of the symptoms. Very helpful too!

name: Dr. Kishore
address: Revive Wellness centre, hebbal

reviews.text: Good

She's patient, and empathetic. She told me she is a neurodivergent person herself and I like her way of curious exploration. She uses CBT and mindfulness techniques. Takes her time to understand and listen to your problems (which might not be perceived as good by some, but is effective). She is also connected to a psychiatrist who practices online so you can also get access to medication. She has a broader perspective than just diagnosis and labeling. I had an awesome experience and would totally recommend her.

name: Dr. Krishna
address: Revive hebbal

name: Dr. Shantanu Bharti
address: Medanta lucknow

reviews.text: I mean it was good he prescribed me inspiral and after a while prescribed me concerta.

name: Dr. Akshada Amonkar

reviews.text: Very friendly and prescribed stimulant meds, no stigma.

name: Dr. Jateen Ukrani
address: The Lifestyle Clinic, Market no. 1, K1/104, First Floor, Beside OYO, Near Kamla Sweets, Chittaranjan Park-1, Delhi

reviews.text: The doctor was really nice and understanding. We explored other options before coming to the ADHD diagnosis as well. He also explained other issues which could be byproduct of ADHD like anxiety and depression. A test was conducted to confirm the ADHD diagnosis. He did not recommend stimulant medication at the start because of some family history. We started with non stimulants and continued for about 2 or 3 months before switching to stimulant medication. He also recommends counseling therapy alongwith the medication to help deal with ADHD better. He's overall very friendly and reassuring.
She is a neurodiverse-affirmative therapist.
She was thorough with her reports and analysis. Took a couple of sessions. I was referred by my psychiatrist for a standard evaluation before she could start any meds.

name: Dr. Debanjan Banerjee
address: Apollo Multispecialty Hospital, Mukundapur & Resurrection, Mental Healthcare Hospital, Chak Garia

reviews.text:
He's young, updated doctor from NIMHANS, knows about adhd, asks very crisp, to the point qs regarding symptoms, might want to corroborate via parents, will try to analyze and cross out other possible diagnoses like ocd, definitely open to stimulant medication, kinda brusque but will listen carefully. Ask for stimulant medication supplier if prescribed. (Did try to confirm if my adhd was from childhood or adulthood) (also probably knows about inattentive adhd since he did gave me the diagnosis on 2nd consultation) 
Contact no is of his secretary who'll book the offline visits.

name: Dr. NK Tak
onlinePlatform: Practo, but you can call him.
address: 168 A, Sector 5, Patrakar colony, New Sanganer, Road, Mansarovar, Jaipur, Rajasthan 302020

reviews.text: I found the doctor to be pretty smart, he doesn't judge you or anything. Also he seems to be in the know of research about it and does have a good understanding of adult adhd. I am inattentive myself so he does recognize me for it. Also for stimulants, he will give you if need be. I think he might even prescribe them online if he finds it necessary but you have to talk to him.

name: Dr. Gajanan Kulkarni
address: Apollo hospital, jayanagar bangalore

reviews.text: He was the person I went to first to get diagnosed for adult adhd and he mentioned having more patients like me. I never to meet him personally since I live too far. There's a nice review about him on reddit and I found him through that post. I think he does give stimulants but follows the set protocol.

name: Dr. Sheenam Goyal
address: mindcare neuropsychiatry clinic, A4/34 First Floor Paschim Vihar Delhi, 110063 India

reviews.text:
informed and contemporary(!) understanding of particularly female/afab adult adhd with asd traits, with depression and anxiety episode. received atomoxetine and other very helpful psychiatric prescriptions. The psychological approach was not up to date however, the Dr. insisted on accompanying therapy which is fine but that psychologist lacking overview, sensitivity and proper understanding; offering rather utilitarian and counterproductive CBT following textbook rather than actual needs: "you have to get over your procrastination and answer your emails this week" was horrible advice while I was in hospital and therefore had to learn to rest, take it easy and prioritise my health over work - the exact opposite.
I would seek out this Doctor any time again for great remote consultation and diagnosis, and for therapy proceed only if they offer something more reasonable than CBT. which could already be the case, my sessions were at the end of 2022.

Sheenam Goyal has been deeply considerate of my circumstances, creating a gentle and non-judgmental space where I’ve been able to express concerns that I often struggle to articulate. She has shown genuine understanding of my condition and has consistently acknowledged the challenges I face when navigating neurotypical environments, as well as the unique hurdles that come with being neurodivergent.

While she initially expressed a preference for not relying on stimulant medication long-term out of concern that it could be habit-forming, this was approached thoughtfully rather than dogmatically. I shared my personal experience with stimulants and clarified that this concern has not aligned with my lived reality. She understood my perspective clearly, without dismissiveness, resistance, or opposition, and engaged with it in an open and balanced manner.

She has an open and informed understanding of both ADHD and autism, based on the neuroscience behind these conditions rather than general assumptions. She recognizes that these conditions affect individuals differently and is attentive to the specific challenges that often come with ADHD, approaching them with openness and care.

name: Dr. Krishna Thalagavara

reviews.text: He is a great listener and helps us take things step by step, he also advices career options for people with ADHD and will help and educate us on everything ADHD

reviews.text: Got online consultation over whatsapp, afterwards he referred to me a psychologist who took the test. The process was over within 2 hours. However, he prescribed the med only in person visit. Behaviour of the doctor was great. He also has a youtube channel “ABeautifulMindClinic”.

reviews.text:
Maybe not a specialist, but he's the most bright and compassionate psychiatrist that I have seen.
He gives time to his patients and listens to the research one might have done online. He also gives good practical non-medicinal advice. He gives medicines according to one's present symptoms and difficulties and is willing to try different medicines if one doesn't work. If you trust him and have proper rapport, he will most definitely(with time) provide the medications that you require, even if it is methylphenidate.

~filled by Impulse on behalf of an anonymous commenter.

She is amazing. Not just knowledgeable about ADHD but gave me the most helpful resources that cater to me instead of cookie cutter "make a planner and exercise" stuff. She is so so knowledgeable, very intuitive about what you need, and I've seen her qualifications and they are crazy good. She's constantly learning as far as I can tell. She is very observant and perceptive. Highly recommended

I was a bit skeptical of going for solution-focused treatment because it often felt invalidating and hard to keep up with, but therapy with Chetna as always been helpful and she carried it at a pace that was more comfortable with me. She is quite well-informed about ADHD and keeps up with relevant research. There have been no issues so far (it's been 5 months) and it's been immensely productive, and I felt understood no matter what came up.

reviews.text: He's old , he's Consultant Psychiatrist and Head, Psychiatry Unit at KEM Hospital so pretty good at what he does. Whenever I visit him I never feel like I'm a patient he makes me feel like I'm his friend. He hears my rants, sometimes we talk for over half hour about something completely unrelated things. He has been helpful to me. And I owe him a lot for everything that he has done for me. He doesn't like to prescribe stimulant medications, but overall he is a good doc.

name: Ankita Deshmukh

reviews.text: She is a great therapist no stigma. Thanks to her I'm exposing myself to world and dealing with my anxiety and depression in a good way. Also about ADHD she has a extensive knowledge, and whenever I come up with something new she genuinely researches about it and then guide me through it. She is more than a therapist for me she is someone whom I can text about something that happened in my life and despite her busy schedule she'll make a slot available for me. Due to my ADHD I zone out or get distracted and she let me be myself, when I run late she's like "take your time just drive safe". Sometimes my session time exceeds and she let's me vent without charging extra. Overall a great therapist.

name: Ms Kavita Purohit.
address: D-502, Jyoti Arch, S.V. Road, Kandivali(West), Mumbai – 400067

reviews.text: I've been to her twice via offline consultation. The first time I was nervous and severely anxious and depressed. She talked me through it for 2 whole hours and was very encouraging and nice. I showed her my previous ADHD diagnosis and in the next session she conducted her own test, and concluded that the test I'd already done was correct. She is very positive about living with ADHD as her own kid has ADHD. She quickly understood my depression stems from my untreated ADHD, and guided me to get medication. She does not provide medication herself. She provides therapy, cognitive reframing. She's warm, gentle yet practical. I'm happy to consult with her. The number provided is the reception number. I don't know if she provides online consultation. Her website is - https://www.kavitapurohit.in/

I found her through Fettle Counseling Services. It was a really good experience because she is very approachable and not just listens but understands you as well. I found her to hold a right balance between friendly/reassuring and yet thorough with her questioning.

name: Dr. Manvee Chaudhary (Manas Mind Clinic)
address: ground floor 1, Plot no 14, Mayur Vihar, Niti Khand 2, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh 201014

reviews.text:
Empathetic and actively listened to what I had to describe about my experiences with executive dysfunction, impulsivity, cognitive functioning, and other impairments. She validated me by suggesting that she too would guess that I have inattentive ADHD. The doctor didn't demonstrate any stigma towards "adult ADHD" and at least did not mention anything against stimulant medications. 

She showed a great attitude towards Adult ADHD, at least compared to other psychiatrists I tried in the past who on the other hand were extremely impatient to dismiss and invalidate my lived experiences and who authoritatively claimed that I can't have ADHD as an adult and that I just have common depression. Not Dr. Manvee, though! She understood where I was coming from, and she gave me the space to advocate for myself and let me draw the contrast between depression and executive dysfunction; being very receptive of what the patients tell her.

name: Dr. Wilona Annunciation (Braganza)
address: Catalysts Clinic G1-G2, Jeevan Apartments, Madanlal Dhingra Road, Panchpakhadi, Thane, West. 400602

reviews.text:
She charges 2000 for 30 minutes and does 1-2 days a week at IPH Thane at reduced charges

Dr Wilona was my very first psychiatrist in IPH Thane. Her initial choice of medication for me was SPOT on! Back then I didn't have any Autism or ADHD diagnosis. Because every time I went off that medication, I got less productive and my life suffered due to emotional dysregulation. Although my own ADHD diagnosis came from another psychiatrist in Pune, their medicines were useless and their advice ended up ruining 1.5 years of my life. I went back to Dr Wilona and we restarted same medication and have recently started on Inspiral too. She accepted my ADHD diagnosis after looking at my reports (soft copy sent on WhatsApp) and my Autism diagnosis came long before that because at that time, I was still with her.

She is very friendly and very attentive to what I am trying to say. As ADHDers, we always struggle with maintaining focus during the length of the session. So we need the MHP to maintain that focus. She helps maintain that focus and I can also do the rest.

I think she does have a good understanding of Adult ADHD because she at least one other Adult ADHD patient who's my friend and who also has at least one medication that's same to mine. I think that one medication (oxcarbzapine) is extremely helpful to tackle the emotional dysregulation part of ADHD. I think Dr. Wilona has a profound understanding of why that medicine works for ADHD because she told me she uses it for her other neurodivergent patients too.

Having said that, I only suspected ADHD on my own and only while I was not working with her. I never mentioned the word "focus" during any of my psychiatry sessions (not until 2 months after my diagnosis_ and she never picked up on it either while we were working together before that diagnosis. She is probably not as proactive as she should be so the patient has to be proactive on their own. She also didn't order my vitamin deficiency tests until 6 months after we started (back in late 2018 early 2019). And even then I had to proactively request her for that. When asked my psychologist at that time, he said it was assumed that that's what patients get that deficiency tested first. I had massive D3 and B12 deficits, for which she prescribed tests on my request and then medication too.

Having said that, she has many loyal patients from across many Indian states and her chemist in Thane clinic can also courier certain medications if the patients have trouble collecting it from their local medical shops. And I am one of those loyal patients too.

She also has 97% on Practo. 

She has no stigma about stimulant medication that I saw, but she only dispenses Inspiral from her own office inside her own clinic (that's why I travel from Pune to Mumbai for a day to get my Inspiral as and when I need). She has kept her sensitive medicines under a double lock inside her office and her chemist has the rest. She even asks patients to return any unused strips back to ensure they're not misused and writes her own prescriptions carefully.

She is also available on Whatsapp to answer SOME of my questions related to meds but not all the time and within reasonable limits. Her assistants are definitely available on Whatsapp during working hours to set appointments (that number is shared above)

name: Dr. Vinay Saini
address: Karuna healing mind, Kamla hospital, sector 37 d, kadipur, Gurgaon

reviews.text: Very good, very knowledgeable,

name: Antonio D'Costa, Paediatrician(Trauma-Informed), E R Therapist
address: Happy Kids Clinic, Vasco/Cansaulim. Also, attached to Daffodils hospital, Vasco. But EMDR teleconsultations are mostly online, international availability.

reviews.text:
😅
I'll be describing myself.
I'm pretty friendly and get along with kids well.
I'm neurodiverse myself.
I have a good understanding of ADHD having worked with a lot of kids and knowing a lot of adults with it. Executive dysfunction, RSD, time blindness, masking being some important points.
Yes, I can recognise inattentive ADHD
No stigma about any medication, therapy, gender, race, culture, sexual orientation.

I'll be clear, I am ONLY an EMDR therapist, so I work on the trauma part of ADHD, both in kids and adults.

I do provide free/50% off/reduced rate sessions. Those can be directly booked from the website. I was granted a 1.5L scholarship by a US organisation for my EMDR training for social welfare work I do in my rural setup and to further the reach of trauma therapy.

name: Dr. Smriti Mahajan
address: Healing Hospital and Institute of Paramedical Sciences, Sector 34A Chandigarh

reviews.text: Very well researched - she's done research on methylphenidate too. Friendly enough, nothing crazy but I had a positive interaction with her. Listens to issues and does not have stigma around stimulant medication. Recognises inattentive ADHD. Did not shove antidepressants, SSRIs and anti anxiety meds onto me, she identified that those were resultant from my lifelong ADHD. If you know what you're doing and go in with the right evidence, and hopefully someone to vouch for you, then she should be able to get you the help you need. Very fair and gradual titration process.

She is friendly, makes you feel comfortable tries to recognize subtle patterns as well.
She had a decent enough understanding of adhd and also was receptive to my inputs. 
She was ready to prescribe stimulants but was quite cautious in doing so, seems more on the cautious side.

name: Apoorva Yadav
address: Centre for behavioral sciences, amar colony

reviews.text: They seemed to understand the lived experience of adhd. It was a gentle and affirming approach which I needed. The place where they work has a psychiatrist who diagnoses as well. Got the assessment and medication at the same place. She was quite pro medication which was a first for a therapist.

name: Dr. Poorva Sahasrabuddhe
address: VIVEKA Counseling and Healing, Senapati Bapat Rd, Chaitanya 25/A, Hanuman Nagar, Wadarvadi, Pune, Maharashtra 411016, India

reviews.text: She was very friendly and helps we throughout my ADHD and i have ADHD- PI type.

name: Vaidehee - Therapist
address: Doc Thakur Clinc, 5th floor bodycare complex, vadaj, Ahemdabad

reviews.text: She is a psychiatrist plus gives therapy. She has a superb understanding of ADHD, Trauma, Autism, OCD, and all other co-morbidities. She takes a lot of data from you to understand and give you the right care. She is patient, empathetic, practical, and non judgemental. Her therapy helped me process real-time distress like I never was able to before. It stopped my panic attacks and actually rewired my brain. I was surprised and thrilled. She will profile you very accurately from your relationships to your psyche and more. She doesn’t have a one size fits all approach, instead she tailors the therapy according to who you are. She takes her time analysing your reactions to medications only then recommending the right tweaks and dosages. And you can air out all your silly concerns as well she will be glad to answer everything. For all my girlies out there, she is especially great! I was able to tell her about my PMDD like symptoms and how meds stop working during some parts of the menstruation cycle. She listened and adapted to that as well, recommending changes in medication for that too. She looked at my past reports and identified something I'd missed that could be causing inflammation in my body. She takes a holistic approach by looking at past diseases/infections/diagnoses you have and recommending exercise, sleep aides, vitamins etc apart from the psychiatric medications. Truly a professional that the world needs more of. A standard apart from all the other professionals I've been with before.

name: Vaidehee Chaudhary - Therapist
address: Doc Thakur Clinc, 5th floor bodycare complex, vadaj, Ahemdabad

reviews.text: I've been to many therapists/psychiatrists before but Dr Vaidehee has set the standard apart from everybody. She's really invested in your health from mental to physical aspects as she understands the interplay between them. After beginning her sessions I have found the changes in medications to be exactly what my body needs. She reintroduced stimulants in my stack after I thought they didn’t work for me, and suprisingly at low doses they are actually doing wonders for me! She takes her time and analyses the data given to her to really understand what might be the best fit. Her therapy has addressed the core issues of my psyche, helping me cope with distress in a manner I've never been able to before. She is easy to talk to, empathetic, understanding, and non judgemental. Strict where she needs to be. Truly the world needs more people like her.

name: Dr. Shyam Mithiya
address: Shreyas Signal, 86 Central, 203, Andheri Ghatkopar Link Rd, Gangawadi, Ghatkopar West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400086

reviews.text: To the point, questions asked and correct diagnosis.

name: Dr. Harikrishan Mamtani
address: Office Wing, Dr Harkishan Mamtani MD Psychiatry (NIMHANS)- Manoved, Ramdaspeth, Pushpakunj Complex, Manoved- Centre for Mental Well Being and Research, AGM Multispecialty Clinic, 6A/6B, 1st floor, near Centre Point Hotel, Ramdaspeth, Nagpur, Maharashtra 440010

reviews.text:
The doctor is well informed, friendly and flexible. Open to feedbacks and opinions. In my case, he would keep opinions and preferences in mind while working on case. 

I'm on stimulants as of now. I was prescribed stimulant online but had to get someone to collect the prescription in person. That said, I'm from same city which might be why this happened. You guys can talk to them and see how they can accommodate your situation.

name: Dr. Tushar Kant from Fortis(Vaishali Nagar)

reviews.text: He was very open and understanding for the Adult ADHD.

name: Dr. Srinivas Singisetti

reviews.text:
Super friendly 
Very open to hearing you out 
Good understanding on ADHD, Autism, Neurodivergence
No stigma
Lot of empathy

name: Dr. Varsha Dawani
address: Opp Pappu garden, near pappu Society, behind Town Hall, Ulhasnagar, Maharashtra 421002

reviews.text: Friendly, Good Listener, Supportive.

name: Dr. Bhavneet Kaur
address: Holistic Health and Healing, B 49, Block B, Amar Colony, Lajpat Nagar, Delhi

reviews.text: My experience with Dr Bhavneet was/is amazing. She listens for as long as one wants to speak, is approachable, has a good understanding of ADHD and adult ADHD (although I do not know if she's an adult ADHD specialist), recognises inattentive ADHD (I myself am diagnosed with ADHD-PI), and prescribes stimulant medication after trying out non-stimulants first. She is very thorough and does not jump to assumptions or conclusions. She is also mindful of comorbidities and recognised my mood disorder early enough to prevent a major episode. I am under treatment for both ADHD and a mood disorder (that looks similar to Bipolar-II), and Dr Bhavneet is a relief in this journey.

name: Dr. Sudha Rani
address: Healthyminds clinic, 8th cross, Kalidasa Rd, Vani Vilas Mohalla, Mysuru, Karnataka 570002

reviews.text: Helped my friend hop on atomoxetine, she doesn't prescribe stims and not much knowledge about ADHD, but does recognise ADHD at least, my friend is doing fine

IDK if the doctor prescribes stimulants online, but he provisionally diagnosed my friend from Kolkata and prescribed stims on first visit
